name: Zig MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for the Zig programming language, from openSVM organization. Enables AI agents to interact with Zig toolchain — compiling Zig code, running Zig programs, querying Zig documentation, and supporting AI-assisted systems programming in Zig.
use_cases: Compiling and running Zig code from systems programming agents
Querying Zig stdlib documentation from coding agents
Running Zig build system commands from DevOps agents
Supporting AI-assisted low-level systems development in Zig
not_for: Non-Zig projects — Zig is a niche systems language
Production compilation pipelines without proper sandboxing
best_when: A systems programmer using Zig wants AI agents to compile, run, and interact with Zig code — particularly for embedded/low-level development workflows.
avoid_when: You don't use Zig. The language is growing but still niche — toolchain and API surface may be unstable as Zig approaches 1.0.

auth.notes: Local Zig toolchain — no authentication required. Requires Zig compiler installed locally.

pricing.notes: Zig is free open source. MCP server is free open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Zig language is pre-1.0 — toolchain and stdlib APIs change between versions
Code execution via MCP — ensure sandboxing for arbitrary Zig code compilation
openSVM org is community-level — not official Zig Software Foundation
Zig compilation can be slow for large projects — set appropriate timeouts
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Zig compilation. Code execution risk — sandbox if accepting arbitrary agent-generated code. No external API calls. Local builds only.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local code compilation. Arbitrary code execution risk — sandbox at OS level. No credentials needed.
